creator: dan@langille.org
text:
On Dec 23, 2013, at 2:07 PM, olli hauer <ohauer@gmx.de> wrote:

> On 2013-12-23 19:52, Dan Langille wrote:
>>=20
>> On Dec 23, 2013, at 1:49 PM, olli hauer <ohauer@gmx.de> wrote:
>>=20
>>> On 2013-12-23 19:43, Dan Langille wrote:
>>>> Olli,
>>>>=20
>>>> Thanks.  There is no such file on the affected system:
>>>>=20
>>>> $ ls -l /var/db/pkg/auditfile
>>>> ls: /var/db/pkg/auditfile: No such file or directory
>>>>=20
>>>=20
>>> Hi Dan,
>>>=20
>>> I'm not sure if the name was auditfile or only audit ...
>>> Maybe look with `ls /var/db/pkg/a*=92
>>=20
>> [dan@slocum:~] $ ls -d /var/db/pkg/a*
>> /var/db/pkg/apr-1.4.8.1.5.3           /var/db/pkg/autoconf-2.69       =
      /var/db/pkg/automake-1.14
>> /var/db/pkg/aspell-0.60.6.1_2         =
/var/db/pkg/autoconf-wrapper-20101119 =
/var/db/pkg/automake-wrapper-20130530
>>=20
>=20
> Hm, are you using the pkgng or the old pkg-tools?
>=20
> With the pkgng there should be only some files ...
>=20
> $ find /var/db/pkg/
> /var/db/pkg/
> /var/db/pkg/local.sqlite
> /var/db/pkg/vuln.xml
> /var/db/pkg/repo-local.sqlite
>=20

I think this box uses pkgng:

$ grep PKG /etc/make.conf
#PKGDIR =3D/usr/ports/packages
WITH_PKGNG=3D	yes

$ pkg info -x lib
libXau-1.0.8
libXdmcp-1.1.1
libXfont-1.4.5,1
libcheck-0.9.10
libexecinfo-1.1_3
libffi-3.0.13
libfontenc-1.1.2
libgcrypt-1.5.3
libgpg-error-1.11
libiconv-1.14_1
libpthread-stubs-0.3_3
libtool-2.4.2_2
libxcb-1.9.1
libxml2-2.8.0_2
libxslt-1.1.28
libyaml-0.1.4_2


$ find /var/db/pkg/
/var/db/pkg/
/var/db/pkg/libXau-1.0.8
/var/db/pkg/libXau-1.0.8/+DESC
/var/db/pkg/libXau-1.0.8/+MTREE_DIRS
/var/db/pkg/libXau-1.0.8/+COMMENT
/var/db/pkg/libXau-1.0.8/+CONTENTS
/var/db/pkg/libXau-1.0.8/+REQUIRED_BY
/var/db/pkg/libxslt-1.1.28
/var/db/pkg/libxslt-1.1.28/+MTREE_DIRS
/var/db/pkg/libxslt-1.1.28/+CONTENTS
/var/db/pkg/libxslt-1.1.28/+COMMENT
/var/db/pkg/libxslt-1.1.28/+DESC
/var/db/pkg/libxslt-1.1.28/+REQUIRED_BY

--=20
Dan Langille - http://langille.org
